label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem. So I need to figure out which of the four options is true based on the given passage. Let me start by reading the passage carefully again. The first paragraph talks about the various uses of computers, from solving complex problems to controlling machines. It mentions calculators for simple math, large computers for handling multiple problems, using computers for games, learning languages, and the Kurzweil Reading Machine for blind people. The second paragraph explains how the Kurzweil machine works. A blind person puts the book on the scanner, the machine reads the page, and when the person turns the page, they put it back. The machine can read in normal or fast voice. The last sentence says "can read words with small letters and large letters." The next part says blind people like it because they can read newspapers, magazines, etc., and libraries have them. Also, it can help short-sighted children learn to read. Now, looking at the options: (A) Computers can solve all the problems people can't do. The passage says computers can solve difficult problems, but "all the problems people can't do" is too broad. The text doesn't say they can do every single thing people can't. So A is probably false. (B) Large computers can only solve one problem at a time. The passage states that scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time. So B is the opposite of what's said. So B is false. (C) When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything. The passage says the blind person needs to open the book, put it on the scanner, turn the page and put it back. So they do have to do some actions. Therefore, C is false. (D) The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and
